question_text: Explain why ultraviolet (UV) radiation can cause skin damage, but visible light from the sun cannot, even though both are electromagnetic waves.
correct_answer: UV radiation has a higher frequency (shorter wavelength) than visible light, so each UV photon carries more energy. This higher energy can break chemical bonds in skin cells and damage DNA, causing mutations and skin cancer. Visible light photons do not have enough energy to cause this damage.
explanation: Frequency and energy are linked: higher frequency means higher energy per photon. UV photons are energetic enough to ionise molecules and damage cells, while visible light photons pass through or are absorbed as heat without causing chemical damage. This is why sunscreen protects against UV but not visible light.
